title: schenkia spicata var. tamanica
intro: Schenkia spicata var. tamanica, also known as Tamanu oil tree, is a plant species native to the Pacific Island region, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant's seeds contain oil that is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The oil from the seeds has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions such as eczema and wounds, promoting skin regeneration and reducing inflammation, thereby providing health benefits. The herbal preparations of Tamanu oil involve cold-pressing the seeds to extract the oil, which can be used topically, or mixed with carrier oils for massage therapy and skincare applications.
